findings.description: Dangling cross-reference to nonexistent section. The document states 'Security considerations are documented in Section 6' but the document contains no Section 6. The sections are: Overview, Architecture Components, Performance Characteristics, Operational Notes, Conclusions.
findings.evidence: "Security considerations are documented in Section 6." — No Section 6 exists in the document.
findings.suggestion: Either add a Security section (Section 6) covering the referenced security considerations, or remove the forward reference and incorporate security details inline.

findings.description: Structural contradiction in pipeline execution model. The Overview declares all stages run in parallel, but Stage 2 explicitly states sequential dependency with Stage 3.
findings.evidence: Overview: "All pipeline stages run in parallel to maximize throughput." Stage 2: "Stage 2 must complete before Stage 3 begins."
findings.suggestion: Reconcile the execution model. Either the overview should describe a staged/sequential pipeline with parallelism within stages, or the Stage 2 dependency statement should be revised.

findings.description: Reference to undefined 'standard retry policy'. For a final architecture brief, this is a dangling reference to an unspecified policy that is not defined anywhere in the document.
findings.evidence: "Transformation applies the standard retry policy on transient failures." — No retry policy is defined or referenced elsewhere in the document.
findings.suggestion: Define the retry policy (max retries, backoff strategy, failure escalation) or provide a reference to where it is documented.

summary: Three structural integrity issues found. Two high-severity findings: a dangling reference to a nonexistent Section 6 and a contradiction between the parallel execution claim in the overview and the sequential dependency stated in Stage 2. One medium-severity finding: an undefined 'standard retry policy' referenced without specification. The document's overall structure is sound but these reference and consistency gaps prevent a clean pass for a final artifact.
